headline: While you’re watching the World Cup, the feds may be watching you
summary: Surveillance concerns rise during World Cup and America250 events. Millions of spectators and residents face potential monitoring by authorities. Drones and cameras are deployed in host cities like Kansas City.

headline: This slim camera has a transparent LCD screen for a viewfinder
summary: Point-and-shoot cameras are experiencing a new wave of popularity. Godox introduces a new slim camera with a unique transparent LCD viewfinder. This innovation follows the trend of popular retro and simple cameras.

headline: I finally got my Trump phone
summary: After over a year, The Verge reviewer finally receives the Trump Mobile T1 Phone 8002. The anticipation for the "Trump phone" has been a running gag. First impressions of the gold version are now being shared.

headline: Hydration isn’t complicated: Just drink water
summary: Amidst heatwaves and complex hydration products, a simple truth prevails: just drink water. The "Optimizer" newsletter debunks marketing for unnecessary gizmos and potions. Stay hydrated effectively without overcomplicating it.

headline: People Are Worried They’ll Only See Half of The Odyssey If They Miss Out on Imax, But Should They Be?
summary: Viewers fear missing crucial content if they don't see "The Odyssey" in IMAX. The article questions if these concerns about viewing experience are justified. It explores the importance of the IMAX format for this particular title.

headline: The View From Japan: PlayStation's Push for an All-Digital Future Raises Concern From Gamers and Developers — and a Well-Timed Retweet From Hideo Kojima
summary: PlayStation announced an all-digital future for games from January 2028, ending disc production. This move sparks significant backlash from Japanese gamers and developers alike. Game preservation and ownership are key concerns, echoed by Hideo Kojima.

headline: The Complicated History of GTA Controversies
summary: Grand Theft Auto has a long and complex history of controversies. Issues range from moral panics and celebrity lawsuits to hidden game content. This deep dive explores the various scandals surrounding the popular franchise.

headline: 'Significant' Pay Rises Offered by Professor Layton Studio — if You Correctly Answer Quizzes About the Company's Games
summary: Level-5, the studio behind Professor Layton, offers substantial pay raises. Employees must pass quizzes about the company's games to qualify. This unique incentive tests employee knowledge and dedication to their titles.

headline: Police criticise timing of decision on pubs staying open for England match
summary: Police criticize the late notice for extended pub opening hours during the England match. The decision forces officers away from regular duties to manage crowds. National Police Chiefs' Council expresses concerns over resource allocation.

headline: Three men found not guilty of journalist Lyra McKee's murder
summary: Three individuals were acquitted of journalist Lyra McKee's murder. McKee, 29, was shot during rioting in Londonderry in April 2019. The verdict brings a conclusion to a high-profile case.

headline: On the Strait of Hormuz, BBC finds seized ships and shark fishermen as uneasy calm returns
summary: BBC journalists gained rare access to the Iranian side of the Strait of Hormuz. They observed seized ships and local shark fishermen amidst a fragile peace. The visit marks the first time a UK broadcaster reported from the region.

headline: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
summary: Police are searching for a Ukrainian suspect in a Monaco bomb attack. Officials believe the individual, who was disguised as a man, may not have acted alone. The suspect reportedly spent days casing the attack scene.
